cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what a person who enjoys the arts may be able to do, based on the passage. The options are A through D. First, I'll need to refer back to the passage. The main points discussed are that people who engage with the arts from a young age have better jobs and higher salaries. The passage then explains why—because art study helps with reasoning, problem-solving, creative and critical thinking. It also mentions that art excites imagination and makes you think, and that through art, you can see the world from different perspectives. For example, looking at Michelangelo's art makes you understand his faith, Picasso shows life in new ways, Shakespeare teaches about character, etc. The passage also states that the arts help develop the ability to see things from different points of view, which helps in communicating and cooperating with others. So the key here is that art encourages understanding different perspectives. Looking at the options: A) see things from different points of view — this matches what the passage says directly. B) deal with problems worse than others — the passage says the opposite, that art helps with problem-solving, so B is incorrect. C) be difficult to communicate with others — again,
